commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: The mouth of one speaking ill is a great pit, a steep precipice for the innocent, but steeper for one of ill will. An innocent person, though easily credulous, falls quickly, but when he has fallen rises again. The slanderer is thrown headlong by his own acts, from which he will never emerge or escape.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
It. Debauchery resembles hell, chap. xxiii. 23. (Calmet)
Facilis descensus Averni (Virgil, Æneid vi.)
